QF.id: VI Debt Instruments and Government Securities_47

question.text: By when must the depository distribute interest on government securities to clients after RBI makes it available?

answer.text: Before the end of day (EOD) of the interest payment due date
Within 3 working days of the interest payment due date
Within 7 working days of receiving from RBI
Within 30 calendar days of the payment due date

explanation.text: **The Logic:** The depository should distribute the interest to clients before the EOD of the interest payment due date, after RBI has made it available. **The Trap:** Longer timelines seem more practical but the requirement is same-day distribution before EOD. **Cross-Exam Context:** Interest payment timelines for G-Secs are tested in NISM Series VI. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** G-Sec interest should reach clients on the payment date itself — if there's a delay, follow up with the depository immediately.
